applied.negative_prompt: readable text, letters, numbers, serial numbers, labels, label-like marks, document text, chart text, diagram text, whiteboard writing, UI, captions, subtitles, poster design, slogans, check marks, mission patches, logos, NASA logo, agency insignia, flags, flag-like colored patch, colored cloth, Shuttle markings, Challenger wordmark, copied archival image, copied diagram, exact historical room, named-person likeness, recovered hardware trophy, complete O-ring, small complete ring, rubber gasket ring, tabletop full field joint, full SRB field joint model, full launch tower, full launch pad silhouette, wide launch pad establishing shot, visible vehicle, vehicle identity, hero launch image, triumph imagery, courtroom scales, villain tableau, product photography, jewelry, glossy product macro, glowing seam, orange glow, red glow, flame path, smoke jet, plume, explosion, fireball, debris scene, bodies, over-blue color grade, blue monochrome, heavy ice fantasy, theatrical fog, dramatic haze, fog, psychedelic, surreal floating parts, multiple symbolic events, clutter, warped geometry, generic charred debris, torn cloth, loose soot fragments, ash smear, soot cloud, trophy hardware
applied.positive_prompt: restrained editorial evidence still, source-grounded material carrier, one clear subject, controlled neutral light, calm frame, no spectacle, Vertical restrained late-night institutional still of a generic teleconference table with two plain unbranded desk phones and a blank engineering recommendation packet placed apart from a larger blank process folder, the objection enters the process without showing people, exact rooms, readable memos, quotes, logos, whiteboard writing, or agency marks.

notes: Derived from the official ComfyUI Flux.2 Dev text-to-image workflow.
Adapted to expose negative_prompt, batch_size, seed, and filename_prefix for this lab.
The negative prompt branch is retained as an unconnected CLIPTextEncode node because the official FLUX.2 Dev text-to-image path is positive-guidance-first.
Export-time model loader filenames are patched from FluxLab runtime config so local-safe checkpoints can replace the official quantized default.
